title: Peloton Interactive (PTON) Q4 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Peloton Q4 2023 Earnings Call Key Takeaways. Financial Performance & Outlook: CEO Barry McCarthy expressed cautious optimism about the business trajectory, noting the company expects positive free cash flow in the back half of fiscal 2024. However, he acknowledged uncertainty about what's driving recent sales momentum. The company projects strong Q4 performance with double-digit revenue growth and 40-plus percent gross margins, contingent on hitting intermediate quarter targets. Growth Initiatives: App Strategy over 900,000 downloads with roughly 600,000 new-to-platform users. The company is investing in personalization and interface improvements to boost conversion to paid tiers. Brand Partnerships: Co-branding deals announced with Liverpool FC and University of Michigan, with McCarthy emphasizing these will be multiyear, global, exclusive arrangements. Fitness-as-a-Service expected to grow over 70% year-over-year from its current run rate. International Expansion Germany rental program launched August 9th; expansion planned for UK and Canada. Challenges: The seat post recall created headwinds, with roughly 15,000-20,000 incremental paused subscribers attributed to the issue. Management expects churn to normalize once replacements are distributed by September. Hardware gross margins remain pressured, particularly for Q1, as the company balances pricing with subscriber growth objectives.
